questions.q: What was Mangal Pandey's occupation in the service of the British East India Company?
questions.explanation: Mangal Pandey was a sepoy, an Indian soldier serving in the East India Company's Bengal Army.

questions.q: In which year did Mangal Pandey's act of defiance against his British officers take place?
questions.explanation: His attack on British officers occurred on 29 March 1857.

questions.q: Which new rifle's greased cartridges sparked Mangal Pandey's protest?
questions.explanation: The grievance centred on the cartridges for the new Pattern 1853 Enfield rifle.

questions.q: What was the fate of Mangal Pandey after his act of rebellion?
questions.explanation: Mangal Pandey was hanged on 8 April 1857.

questions.q: At which military cantonment was Mangal Pandey stationed when he rebelled?
questions.explanation: Mangal Pandey was posted at the Barrackpore cantonment near Calcutta.

questions.q: Mangal Pandey's defiance is widely seen as a prelude to which larger event?
questions.explanation: His act is regarded as an early spark of the broader Revolt of 1857.

questions.q: What was the religious objection many sepoys had to the greased cartridges?
options: They were said to be coated with cow and pig fat
questions.explanation: The cartridges were rumoured to be greased with cow and pig fat, offending both Hindu and Muslim soldiers who had to bite them open.

questions.q: Which army of the East India Company did Mangal Pandey belong to?
questions.explanation: He served in the Bengal Army of the East India Company.

questions.q: On what date was Mangal Pandey executed?
questions.explanation: He was hanged on 8 April 1857, ahead of the date originally scheduled.

questions.q: How is Mangal Pandey popularly remembered in Indian history?
options: As an early martyr of the freedom struggle
questions.explanation: He is remembered as one of the earliest martyrs and symbols of resistance in India's freedom struggle.

questions.q: Which regiment did Mangal Pandey serve in at Barrackpore?
questions.explanation: He was a sepoy of the 34th Bengal Native Infantry.

questions.q: On 29 March 1857, what did Mangal Pandey call on his fellow sepoys to do?
questions.explanation: He urged his comrades to revolt against their British superiors during the incident on the parade ground.

questions.q: Which British officer did Mangal Pandey wound during the confrontation on the parade ground?
questions.explanation: Lieutenant Baugh was among the officers Mangal Pandey attacked and wounded that day.

questions.q: When ordered to arrest Mangal Pandey, what did the Indian guard commander reportedly refuse to do?
options: Fire on him or seize him
questions.explanation: Jemadar Ishwari Prasad and the guard reportedly refused to arrest or fire on Pandey, an act for which the officer was later executed.

questions.q: What did Mangal Pandey attempt to do when he realised he would be captured?
questions.explanation: He turned his musket on himself in an attempt to take his own life, but survived the wound and was captured.

questions.q: As punishment for the Barrackpore incident, what action did the British take against Mangal Pandey's regiment?
options: It was given a new flag
questions.explanation: The 34th Bengal Native Infantry was disbanded by the British in the aftermath.

questions.q: How did the new Enfield cartridges have to be prepared before loading, which fuelled the sepoys' anger?
options: Their ends had to be bitten off
questions.explanation: Soldiers had to bite off the greased ends of the cartridges, bringing the offensive grease into contact with their mouths.

questions.q: Near which major colonial city was the Barrackpore cantonment located?
questions.explanation: Barrackpore lay just north of Calcutta, then the capital of British India.

questions.q: Mangal Pandey's original execution was scheduled for a later date, but the British advanced it. Why?
options: They feared the example would spark wider mutiny
questions.explanation: Fearing that the unrest would spread among the sepoys, the British carried out the hanging earlier than first planned.

questions.q: In which present-day Indian state is Barrackpore, the site of Mangal Pandey's revolt, located?
questions.explanation: Barrackpore is in present-day West Bengal.

questions.q: The grievance over the greased cartridges reflected a broader fear among sepoys that the British intended to do what?
questions.explanation: Many sepoys believed the cartridges were part of a design to defile their faith and break their caste, deepening the discontent.

questions.q: Mangal Pandey's surname later gave rise to a derogatory British term for rebellious sepoys. What was it?
questions.explanation: British soldiers came to refer to rebel sepoys generally as 'Pandies', derived from Mangal Pandey's name.

questions.q: Mangal Pandey was born in 1827 in a village in which district of present-day Uttar Pradesh?
questions.explanation: He is generally held to have been born in the Ballia district (his birthplace is often cited as Nagwa village) in 1827.

questions.q: Which Indian officer of the guard was court-martialled and executed for refusing to arrest Mangal Pandey?
questions.explanation: Jemadar Ishwari Prasad was hanged in April 1857 for refusing to act against Pandey.

questions.q: On the day of the incident, which sepoy actually helped restrain Mangal Pandey, going against him?
questions.explanation: Sheikh Paltu intervened and helped seize Mangal Pandey during the confrontation, and was later rewarded by the British.

questions.q: Which sergeant-major was among the British personnel attacked by Mangal Pandey on 29 March 1857?
questions.explanation: Sergeant-Major James Hewson was assaulted along with Lieutenant Baugh during the incident.

questions.q: At Barrackpore, an earlier disturbance over the cartridges in the same period affected which other regiment, leading to its disbandment in 1857?
questions.explanation: The 19th Bengal Native Infantry, which had refused the cartridges at Berhampore, was disbanded shortly before the Barrackpore events.

questions.q: The 2005 Hindi film dramatising Mangal Pandey's life carried which title?
questions.explanation: The Ketan Mehta film 'Mangal Pandey: The Rising', starring Aamir Khan, was released in 2005.

questions.q: Because of the swift British reaction, why did no ordinary hangman initially carry out Mangal Pandey's execution?
options: Local hangmen refused, so executioners were summoned from elsewhere
questions.explanation: Accounts record that local hangmen refused the task, and executioners had to be brought in from another station to carry out the hanging.

questions.q: In what way has the Indian government honoured Mangal Pandey's memory in modern times?
options: By minting a gold coin in his image
By issuing a commemorative postage stamp in 1984
By building his tomb at the Red Fort
questions.explanation: India issued a commemorative postage stamp in his honour in 1984, recognising him as a freedom-struggle martyr.
